Catch and Translate RTMB Errors into User-Friendly Messages

Source: R/model.R
with_rtmb_error_handling.Rd

This internal helper function wraps an R expression in a [tryCatch()] block to capture cryptic error messages generated by the RTMB automatic differentiation engine or C++ backend, translating them into descriptive hints.

Usage

with_rtmb_error_handling(expr, block_name)

Arguments

expr

An R expression to be evaluated, typically representing the logic within a specific model block.

block_name

A character string identifying the context of the error (e.g., "parameters", "model", "transform", or "generate").

Value

The result of evaluating the expr. If an error occurs, it throws a formatted [stop()] message.

Details

It uses regular expressions to categorize common RTMB failure modes, such as:

  • **Missing Objects/Functions**: Detects if a variable or a specific distribution function (e.g., `normal_lpdf`) is missing from the environment.

  • **Index Errors**: Identifies subscript out of bounds errors.

  • **AD Type Destruction**: Catches the "lost class attribute" error, often caused by incorrect initialization of vectors (e.g., using `numeric(N)` and partial assignment inside loops).

  • **Matrix Dimension Mismatch**: Identifies non-conformable arguments in matrix multiplication.

On this page
